title: Clone Graph
prompt:
Given a reference of a node in a connected undirected graph, return a deep copy (clone) of the graph.

The graph is represented as an adjacency list. Each node has a value (integer) and a list of its neighbors. The input is given as an adjacency list where adj[i] is a list of neighbors for node i+1 (1-indexed). Your function receives the adjacency list and should return a new adjacency list representing the cloned graph.

Nodes are numbered from 1 to n. The adjacency list has exactly n entries. The graph is connected and has no duplicate edges or self-loops.

For example, given adj = [[2,4],[1,3],[2,4],[1,3]], this represents a 4-node graph where node 1 connects to 2 and 4, node 2 connects to 1 and 3, etc. The output should be an identical adjacency list.
constraints: There are no duplicate edges or self-loops in the graph

stdin.python: r = solve([[2,4],[1,3],[2,4],[1,3]]); print([sorted(x) for x in r])
r = solve([[2],[1]]); print([sorted(x) for x in r])
r = solve([[2,3],[1,3],[1,2]]); print([sorted(x) for x in r])

notesForAI.coreIdea: BFS or DFS with a hash map mapping original node to its clone. For each visited node, create a clone and copy its neighbor list. The hash map prevents infinite loops and ensures each node is cloned exactly once.
commonBugs: Shallow copy instead of deep copy (just copying references)
Infinite loop from not tracking visited nodes
Not handling the single-node case with empty neighbor list
notesForAI.expectedComplexity: O(V + E) where V is nodes and E is edges
